title: Implement SharePoint document download via Graph API
description:
## Context

Download .docx files from SharePoint Online using Microsoft Graph API. Requires auth token from device code flow task.

## Implementation Details

- Parse SharePoint URLs to extract site ID, drive ID, and item ID
  - Handle formats: sharing links, direct URLs, site-relative paths
- Use Graph API endpoints:
  - GET /sites/{siteId}/drives/{driveId}/items/{itemId}/content (download)
  - GET /sites/{hostname}:/path (resolve site by URL)
  - GET /drives/{driveId}/items/{itemId} (metadata)
- Download document to temp directory
- Support SharePoint sharing links (resolve via /shares endpoint)
- Handle large files with streaming download
- Return local file path for conversion pipeline

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Downloads .docx from SharePoint given a URL
- [ ] Handles various SharePoint URL formats
- [ ] Handles sharing links
- [ ] Streams large files without excess memory usage
- [ ] Clear error on permission denied / not found

## Testing

- Unit tests with mocked Graph API responses
- Test URL parsing with various SharePoint URL formats
close_reason: Implemented SharePoint document download via Graph API. Package supports sharing links, direct document URLs, and site-relative paths. Downloads stream to disk for large file support. Typed errors for permission denied and not found. 19 unit tests with mocked Graph API responses.

comments.author: Michael Zalimeni
comments.text: Commit acf9f6d: Implemented sharepoint package with Client.Download(), URL parsing (sharing links, direct URLs, fallback), Graph API integration (/shares, /sites, /drive), streaming download, typed errors. 19 tests all passing.

title: Write Claude Code skill manifest (skill.md)
description:
## Context

Package the tool as a Claude Code skill so it can be invoked as /sharepoint-to-md within Claude Code sessions.

## Implementation Details

- Create skill.md with:
  - Skill name, description, trigger patterns
  - Instructions for Claude Code on how to invoke the binary
  - Parameter documentation
  - Example invocations
- Create install.sh or setup instructions for:
  - Building the Go binary
  - Installing pandoc (brew install pandoc)
  - Azure AD app registration guide (for SharePoint URL mode)
- Add to ~/.claude/skills/ symlink or direct placement

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] skill.md follows Claude Code skill format
- [ ] /sharepoint-to-md invocable from Claude Code
- [ ] Setup instructions are complete and tested

## Notes

Reference existing skills in ~/.claude/skills/ for format conventions.
close_reason: Created SKILL.md with skill name, description, trigger patterns, parameter docs, example invocations, setup instructions (pandoc, Go build, Azure AD app registration), and error handling. Symlinked to ~/.claude/skills/ for /sharepoint-to-md invocation.

comments.author: Michael Zalimeni
comments.text: Commit 2d19c61: Added SKILL.md at .config/claude/skills/sharepoint-to-md/SKILL.md with full skill manifest. Symlinked to ~/.claude/skills/sharepoint-to-md. All tests pass, lint clean.

title: Scaffold Go CLI project with cobra and Makefile
description:
## Context

Set up the Go project structure for sharepoint-to-md with cobra CLI framework and standard Makefile.

## Implementation Details

- Initialize Go module (github.com/user/sharepoint-to-md or internal path)
- Set up cobra root command with flags: --file, --url, --output, --images-dir
- Create Makefile with: build, test, lint, clean targets
- Project layout: cmd/, internal/, main.go
- Add .gitignore for Go binaries

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] go build produces working binary
- [ ] --help shows all flags with descriptions
- [ ] make build/test/lint all work
- [ ] Project structure follows Go conventions
close_reason: Scaffolded Go CLI: cobra root command with --file/--url/--output/--images-dir flags, Makefile with build/test/lint/clean targets, cmd/internal/main.go layout, .gitignore for binary. All acceptance criteria verified: build produces working binary, --help shows all flags, make targets all work, Go conventions followed.

comments.author: Michael Zalimeni
comments.text: Commit 7922301: Scaffolded Go CLI project with cobra root command, flags (--file, --url, --output, --images-dir), Makefile (build/test/lint/clean), and test coverage. All quality gates pass.

title: Implement Microsoft Graph API auth with device code flow
description:
## Context

Enable authenticated access to SharePoint Online documents via Microsoft Graph API. Device code flow is ideal for CLI tools — no web server or redirect URI needed.

## Implementation Details

- Use the Microsoft Authentication Library (MSAL) for Go or direct OAuth2 device code flow
- Register an Azure AD (Entra ID) app with permissions: Files.Read.All, Sites.Read.All
- Implement device code flow: POST to /devicecode endpoint, poll /token endpoint
- Display user-friendly instructions ("Visit https://microsoft.com/devicelogin and enter code XXXXXX")
- Cache tokens to ~/.config/sharepoint-to-md/token.json (with refresh token support)
- Token refresh on expiry without re-prompting
- Support configurable tenant ID (for org-specific apps)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Device code flow prompts user and obtains access token
- [ ] Token cached and reused across invocations
- [ ] Token refresh works transparently
- [ ] Clear error messages for auth failures
- [ ] Configurable client ID and tenant ID via flags or env vars

## Testing

- Integration test with mock OAuth2 server
- Unit tests for token caching/refresh logic

## Notes

Azure app registration is a one-time manual step (documented in README). The skill handles everything after that.
close_reason: Implemented OAuth2 device code flow auth: internal/auth package with token acquisition, disk caching (0600 perms), transparent refresh, mock OAuth2 server tests, auth CLI subcommand, configurable client-id/tenant-id via flags and env vars. All acceptance criteria met.

comments.author: Michael Zalimeni
comments.text: Commit a433503: Implemented device code flow auth in internal/auth package with token caching, refresh, and CLI integration via auth subcommand. All tests pass, lint clean.

title: Wire up end-to-end CLI pipeline
description:
## Context

Connect all components into a cohesive CLI flow: parse args → acquire document → convert → output.

## Implementation Details

- Two code paths based on input:
  1. --file: skip auth, go straight to pandoc conversion
  2. --url: authenticate → download from SharePoint → convert
- Output handling:
  - Default: write to stdout
  - --output flag: write to specified file
  - --images-dir flag: extract images to directory
- Progress feedback for long operations (download, conversion)
- Clean temp file handling (defer cleanup)
- Exit codes: 0 success, 1 conversion error, 2 auth error, 3 network error

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] sharepoint-to-md --file doc.docx produces markdown on stdout
- [ ] sharepoint-to-md --file doc.docx -o out.md writes to file
- [ ] sharepoint-to-md --url "https://..." authenticates and converts
- [ ] Temp files cleaned up on success and failure
- [ ] Meaningful exit codes and error messages

## Testing

- End-to-end test with local .docx file
- Integration test mocking Graph API for URL path
close_reason: Implemented end-to-end CLI pipeline: --file path converts local .docx via pandoc, --url path authenticates via device code flow then downloads from SharePoint before converting. Output goes to stdout by default or to --output file. Structured exit codes (0/1/2/3), temp file cleanup, and comprehensive test coverage.

comments.author: Michael Zalimeni
comments.text: Commit 9f45eec: Wired up end-to-end CLI pipeline. Root command now handles --file (direct pandoc conversion) and --url (auth + download + convert) paths. Added structured exit codes, temp file cleanup, --output file writing, and --images-dir passthrough. Tests cover: no flags (help), both flags (error), non-.docx rejection, missing file, URL without client-id (auth error), ExitError type.

title: Add markdown post-processing and cleanup
description:
## Context

Pandoc output is good but sometimes needs cleanup for production-quality markdown. This task adds optional post-processing.

## Implementation Details

- Strip excessive blank lines (pandoc sometimes adds extra)
- Normalize heading levels if document starts at H3 (shift to H1)
- Clean up table formatting for readability
- Convert absolute image paths to relative
- Optionally strip Word-specific artifacts (e.g., []{.underline} spans)
- Make post-processing opt-in via --clean flag (default: on)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Excessive blank lines removed
- [ ] Heading levels normalized
- [ ] Image paths converted to relative
- [ ] Word artifacts stripped
- [ ] Can be disabled with --no-clean

## Testing

- Unit tests with known pandoc output samples
- Before/after comparison tests
close_reason: Implemented markdown post-processing in internal/cleanup package with 4 transformations (heading normalization, blank line collapsing, Word artifact removal, image path conversion). Integrated via --no-clean flag. 22 unit tests, all passing, linter clean.

comments.author: Michael Zalimeni
comments.text: Commit a4cd073: Added internal/cleanup package with Clean() function implementing heading normalization, blank line collapsing, Word artifact stripping, and image path conversion. Integrated into CLI with --no-clean flag. 22 unit tests, all passing. Linter clean.

title: Implement pandoc-based .docx to .md conversion
description:
## Context

Core conversion engine using pandoc to transform .docx files to markdown.

## Implementation Details

- Shell out to pandoc with appropriate flags: pandoc -f docx -t gfm input.docx
- Use GFM (GitHub Flavored Markdown) as target format
- Handle image extraction: --extract-media flag
- Capture stdout/stderr, handle pandoc errors gracefully
- Check pandoc is installed, provide helpful error if missing
- Support passing through additional pandoc flags via --pandoc-args

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Converts .docx to clean GFM markdown
- [ ] Images extracted to specified directory when requested
- [ ] Tables, lists, headings preserved correctly
- [ ] Clear error when pandoc not installed
- [ ] Handles large documents without hanging

## Testing

- Unit tests with sample .docx files
- Test error cases (missing pandoc, corrupt file, etc.)
close_reason: Implemented pandoc-based .docx to GFM conversion in internal/pandoc package. Covers all acceptance criteria: GFM output, image extraction, extra args passthrough, pandoc-not-installed error, timeout protection for large docs. 16 unit tests + 3 integration tests, lint clean.

comments.author: Michael Zalimeni
comments.text: Commit e3725ff: Implemented internal/pandoc package with Convert() function, CheckInstalled(), configurable Options (InputPath, ImagesDir, ExtraArgs, Timeout), and full test coverage (16 unit tests + 3 integration tests). All quality gates pass.

title: Plan: sharepoint-to-md skill
description:
## Goal

CLI skill that converts SharePoint/Word documents to clean Markdown without losing essential text or formatting.

## Architecture

Two-layer approach:
1. **Document acquisition**: Microsoft Graph API (authenticated SharePoint download) OR local file input
2. **Document conversion**: Pandoc (.docx → .md) as primary engine, with optional image extraction

## Key Design Decisions

- **Pandoc over Google Docs/LibreOffice**: Direct .docx→.md is faster, simpler, and higher quality than multi-hop conversions
- **Microsoft Graph API over Playwright**: API is more reliable, scriptable, and maintainable than browser automation
- **Device code flow for auth**: No web server required, works in CLI context
- **Go CLI**: Matches codebase conventions

## Usage Patterns

```
sharepoint-to-md --file input.docx                          # Local file
sharepoint-to-md --url "https://company.sharepoint.com/..." # SharePoint URL
sharepoint-to-md --url "..." --images-dir ./img             # Extract images
```
close_reason: All subtasks completed — 7 feature issues + 11 fix issues from 3 review iterations

title: Fix checkHTTPError body consumption side effect in sharepoint/download.go
description:
## Context
Review finding (Critical): checkHTTPError in internal/sharepoint/download.go reads resp.Body on error paths, making the body unavailable to callers. This is fragile and could cause silent data loss if error handling flow changes.

## Implementation Details
Refactor checkHTTPError to not consume the response body directly. Options:
- Accept `statusCode int, body []byte` instead of `*http.Response`
- Or have callers read the body first and pass it in
- Or clearly document the body-consumption side effect and ensure callers never read body after checkHTTPError

## Acceptance Criteria
- checkHTTPError does not have hidden side effect of consuming resp.Body
- All callers updated to match new signature
- Existing tests still pass
- No change in external behavior

## Testing
Run `make test` — all sharepoint package tests must pass.
close_reason: Refactored checkHTTPError to eliminate hidden resp.Body consumption side effect. Function now accepts (statusCode int, body []byte). Both callers updated. All tests pass.

comments.author: Michael Zalimeni
comments.text: Commit c11e401: Refactored checkHTTPError to accept (statusCode int, body []byte) instead of *http.Response. graphGet now reads body before checking status; downloadFile reads body only on error. All tests pass.

title: Hoist regex to package-level var in sanitizeFilename
description: regexp.MustCompile is called inside sanitizeFilename on every invocation. Hoist to a package-level var to avoid repeated compilation. In internal/sharepoint/download.go line 310.
close_reason: Hoisted regexp.MustCompile to package-level var unsafeFilenameChars, eliminating per-call recompilation in sanitizeFilename. All tests pass, zero lint issues.

comments.author: Michael Zalimeni
comments.text: Commit 192c06f: Hoisted regexp.MustCompile to package-level var unsafeFilenameChars in download.go. All sharepoint tests pass, 0 lint issues.

title: Add 204 status code test case to TestCheckHTTPError
description: checkHTTPError uses range check (statusCode >= 200 && < 300) but test only covers 200. Add a 204 No Content case to exercise the range logic and prevent regression if someone refactors to == 200.
close_reason: Added 204 No Content test case to TestCheckHTTPError, exercising the 200-299 range check. Tests and lint pass.

comments.author: Michael Zalimeni
comments.text: Commit 9b0a593: Added 204 No Content test case to TestCheckHTTPError table. All tests and linting pass.

title: Fix env var override timing in root.go Execute()
description:
## Context
Review finding (Critical): In cmd/sp2md/root.go Execute(), env var checks run before cobra parses flags. flagTenantID defaults to "common" so SP2MD_TENANT_ID env var never takes effect.

## Implementation Details
Move env var override logic into a PersistentPreRunE on rootCmd. Use cmd.Flags().Changed("tenant-id") to distinguish user-set values from defaults before applying env var fallbacks.

## Acceptance Criteria
- SP2MD_CLIENT_ID env var works when --client-id flag not provided
- SP2MD_TENANT_ID env var works when --tenant-id flag not provided
- Explicit --client-id and --tenant-id flags override env vars
- Tests verify env var behavior

## Testing
Add tests that set env vars and verify flag values after command execution.
close_reason: Fixed env var override timing: moved SP2MD_CLIENT_ID and SP2MD_TENANT_ID fallback logic from Execute() into PersistentPreRunE. Uses cmd.Flags().Changed() to correctly distinguish explicit flags from defaults. All 5 new tests pass, linter clean.

comments.author: Michael Zalimeni
comments.text: Commit f819033: moved env var override logic from Execute() to PersistentPreRunE using cmd.Flags().Changed() to correctly distinguish user-set flags from defaults. Added 5 tests covering env var fallback, flag override, and default preservation.

title: Fix go.mod version from 1.25.0 to actual Go version
description: go.mod specifies go 1.25.0 which does not exist. Set to the actual Go version installed (likely 1.24.x). Run 'go version' to determine correct value.
close_reason: Fixed go.mod version from non-existent 1.25.0 to installed 1.26.0. Tests and linting pass.

comments.author: Michael Zalimeni
comments.text: Commit 6e2c010: Fixed go.mod version from 1.25.0 to 1.26.0. go mod tidy also promoted spf13/pflag to direct dep. All tests pass, 0 lint issues.

title: Add io.LimitReader for unbounded HTTP response reads
description:
## Context
Review finding (Recommendation): All Graph API and Azure AD response bodies are read with io.ReadAll without size limits. A misbehaving server could exhaust memory.

## Implementation Details
Wrap io.ReadAll calls with io.LimitReader for API JSON responses in:
- internal/auth/auth.go (lines 247, 314, 371)
- internal/sharepoint/download.go (lines 232, 250)

Use a reasonable limit like 1MB for JSON API responses.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All io.ReadAll on HTTP response bodies use io.LimitReader
- Limit set to a sensible constant (e.g. 1MB for JSON responses)
- Existing tests pass

## Testing
Run make test.
close_reason: All io.ReadAll(resp.Body) calls wrapped with io.LimitReader at 1MB limit in auth.go and download.go. All tests pass.

comments.author: Michael Zalimeni
comments.text: Commit e9d072c: Wrapped all io.ReadAll(resp.Body) calls with io.LimitReader(resp.Body, 1MB) in auth.go (3 sites) and download.go (2 sites). All tests pass.

title: Handle OAuth slow_down response per RFC 8628
description:
## Context
Review finding (Recommendation): The slow_down OAuth error response is treated identically to authorization_pending. Per RFC 8628 section 3.5, the client MUST increase the polling interval by 5 seconds on slow_down.

## Implementation Details
In internal/auth/auth.go around lines 335-336, when the error is "slow_down", increase the polling interval by 5 seconds before continuing the loop.

## Acceptance Criteria
- slow_down response increases poll interval by 5 seconds per RFC 8628
- Test verifying slow_down behavior
- authorization_pending still uses original interval

## Testing
Add unit test with mock server returning slow_down then authorization_pending.
close_reason: Fixed slow_down handling: requestToken now returns errSlowDown sentinel, pollForToken increases interval by 5s per RFC 8628 §3.5. Added test with mock server verifying backoff timing. All tests and lint pass.

comments.author: Michael Zalimeni
comments.text: Commit e36b31d: fix slow_down handling in pollForToken. Added errSlowDown sentinel, interval += 5s on slow_down, and test verifying the backoff behavior.

title: Add filepath.Base() defense-in-depth to sanitizeFilename
description: In internal/sharepoint/download.go sanitizeFilename (line 301-303), add filepath.Base() call on the sanitized filename as defense-in-depth against path traversal. Current regex preserves dots so '..' could theoretically survive.
close_reason: Added filepath.Base() defense-in-depth to sanitizeFilename with tests for edge cases. All quality gates pass.

comments.author: Michael Zalimeni
comments.text: Commit d049abc: Added filepath.Base() call after regex sanitization in sanitizeFilename. Added test cases for empty string and '..' inputs. All tests and linting pass.

title: Fix data race on pollTimes in TestSlowDownIncreasesInterval
description:
## Context
Review finding (Recommendation): pollTimes []time.Time in TestSlowDownIncreasesInterval is appended from the HTTP handler goroutine and read from the test goroutine without synchronization. Will fail with -race.

## Implementation Details
Option A (preferred): Drop the timing assertion entirely. Rely on poll count + errSlowDown sentinel contract. Timing assertions are inherently flaky in CI.
Option B: Protect pollTimes with sync.Mutex.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Test passes with `go test -race`
- Test still verifies slow_down increases interval (via poll count or mutex-protected timing)

## Testing
Run `go test -race ./internal/auth/...`
close_reason: Removed racy pollTimes slice from TestSlowDownIncreasesInterval. Dropped timing assertion (Option A), relying on atomic poll count to verify slow_down handling. Tests pass with -race, linter clean.

comments.author: Michael Zalimeni
comments.text: Commit fe5a4b4: Removed unsynchronized pollTimes slice from TestSlowDownIncreasesInterval. Used Option A per issue description — dropped timing assertion entirely, relying on poll count (atomic.Int32) to verify slow_down handling. Tests pass with -race, linter clean.

title: Fix sanitizeFilename to return fallback for empty/dot results
description: sanitizeFilename("") returns "." due to filepath.Base(""). Add a guard: if sanitized result is "." or "..", return "file" as fallback. Update the test case expectation from "." to "file".
close_reason: sanitizeFilename now returns "file" fallback for empty/dot results. Guard added for "." and ".." after filepath.Base. Tests updated and passing.

comments.author: Michael Zalimeni
comments.text: Commit c1c3129: Added guard in sanitizeFilename to return "file" fallback when result is "." or "..". Updated test expectations accordingly. All tests pass, zero lint issues.

title: Add table-driven unit tests for checkHTTPError
description:
## Context
Review finding (Recommendation): checkHTTPError changed signature to (int, []byte) but has no direct unit tests. Currently only exercised indirectly through integration tests.

## Implementation Details
Add a table-driven unit test for checkHTTPError covering:
- 401 → ErrPermissionDenied
- 403 → ErrPermissionDenied
- 404 → ErrNotFound
- 500 with body → error message includes body content
- 200 → nil (no error)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Table-driven test covers all status code branches
- Tests pass

## Testing
Run `go test ./internal/sharepoint/...`
close_reason: Added table-driven TestCheckHTTPError with 5 subtests covering all branches: 200→nil, 401→ErrPermissionDenied, 403→ErrPermissionDenied, 404→ErrNotFound, 500→error with body. All tests and lints pass.

comments.author: Michael Zalimeni
comments.text: Commit 292f261: Added table-driven TestCheckHTTPError covering all 5 status code branches (200, 401, 403, 404, 500). All tests pass, zero lint issues.
